title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
When To Consider Buying A Term Insurance Plan?
People may toil day in and day out to earn and provide for their families. They may also save to fulfil their financial goals. However, life is uncertain, and a sudden eventuality might put a stop to their financial plans and goals. Moreover, if the breadwinner of the family passes away, the family might suffer a considerable financial strain. That is where life insurance plans may prove useful. They cover the risk of premature demise and can help the family deal with the financial strain suffered.
When it comes to life insurance plans, term insurance plans are one of the basic forms of coverage. These plans primarily cover the risk of untimely death and help you create a secured corpus for your family. Term plans are cost-effective, allow high coverage levels and give you a sense of financial security. As such, buying a comprehensive term insurance plan may be advisable. But when should you buy the policy?
Buying a Term Insurance Plan – What’s the Right Time?
Your family may start depending on you financially when you become financially independent. So, what may be the right time to buy a term insurance plan?
The answer is – as soon as you can!
Here are the reasons why –
Early Coverage
When you are young, you might be healthy and feel that you don’t need a term insurance policy. But that’s a myth. What about accidental emergencies? Or, if there’s a sudden life-threatening infection or disease, like the recent COVID pandemic?
Age should not be a consideration in delaying a term insurance plan. Even in the prime of health, there might be accidents or illnesses that may prove fatal. That is why buying a term insurance plan at an early age is better. As you buy the plan, you can get early coverage against life's unpredictable challenges.
Lower Premiums
The premium of a term insurance policy depends on age, besides other factors. They may be lower for younger individuals and vice-versa. So, when you buy the plan early on, you might save on the premium cost. You may get lower premiums and also lock in these low rates at higher ages, too, as the premiums don’t usually change during the policy tenure.
Tax Benefits
The premiums paid for term insurance plans qualify for a deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act of 1961. Premiums up to 10%1 of the sum assured, subject to a maximum of Rs.1.5 lakhs1, can be claimed as a deduction under Section 80C1 if conditions of section are satisfied. This benefit is available only in case Old Tax Regime is opted.
The death benefit received from the policy is always tax-free4.
Further Any non-linked policy/policies issued on or after 1 April 2023 with annual premium payable of more than Rs. 5 lakhs (excluding GST), proceeds from such policies will be taxable and income from such policies will be treated as “Income from Other Sources”. If the annual premium is less than Rs. 5 lakhs, income from such policy will continue to be tax free in the hands of policyholder subject to satisfaction of Section 10(10D) conditions.
There is no change in tax treatment for existing policies issued before April 1, 2023. For such polices, if the total premium is more than 5 lakhs, a policyholder is entitled to get all the erstwhile tax benefits, and maturity / surrender /Income benefit proceeds from them will remain tax-free provided the other conditions under Section 10(10D) are satisfied.
However, the death benefit received from the policy is continued to be tax-free@.
When you buy the plan early, you can claim the tax benefits early on and reduce your tax liability.
Peace of Mind
Lastly, buying early also gives you peace of mind. When you have bought a term insurance plan, you know that in the case of an unforeseen contingency, your family would be financially taken care of. This gives you a sense of financial security for your loved ones.
Things to Keep In Mind When Buying A Term Plan
While the aforementioned reasons tell you why you need to consider buying the term plan as soon as possible, there are a few things that you should keep in mind before and after buying the coverage. Some of them are as follows –
Choose an adequate sum assured which would be sufficient to cover your family’s financial needs and goals
Choose a long-term coverage tenure for longer protection
Choose a suitable premium paying term so that the plan becomes affordable
Opt for suitable riders to enhance the scope of your coverage and make it more inclusive
Review your term insurance coverage regularly. If your financial needs increase at the later stages of life, you might need to supplement an existing term insurance coverage. Some term plans allow enhancement of the sum assured at important milestones of life, like marriage or childbirth. Check whether your plan has this option. If not, or if you need to increase the sum assured for another reason, you can opt for another term plan and add to your coverage.
When it comes to buying term insurance, delaying is not the right idea. It may be advisable to buy the coverage early on to protect against life’s unplanned emergencies and for financial security.
Reference
https://cleartax.in/s/80c-80-deductions
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.

full_pdf_text:
When to Consider Buying A Term Insurance Plan?
People may toil day in and day out to earn and make a living for their families. They may also save to fulfil their financial goals. However, life is uncertain, and a sudden eventuality might put a stop to their financial plans and goals. Moreover, if the breadwinner of the family passes away, the family might suffer a considerable financial strain. That is where life insurance plans may prove useful. They cover the risk of premature demise of the life assured and can help the family of the deceased deal with the financial strain suffered.
When it comes to life insurance plans, term insurance plans are one of the basic forms of coverage. These plans primarily cover the risk of untimely death and help you create a secured corpus for your family. Term plans are affordable, allow optimum coverage levels and give you a sense of financial security. As such, buying a comprehensive term insurance plan may be considered. But when should you buy the insurance policy?
Buying a Term Insurance Plan – What’s the Preferred Time?
If you are the sole breadwinner, your family may be dependent on you financially. To ensure they do not have to go through a financial ordeal in your absence, you may consider opting for a term insurance plan today to secure their finances tomorrow.
So, what may be the right time to buy a term insurance plan? The answer is – as early as you can!
Here are the reasons why –
1. Early Coverage
When you are young, you might be healthy and feel that you don’t need a term insurance policy. But that’s a myth. What about accidental emergencies? Or, if there’s a sudden life-threatening infection or disease, like the recent COVID pandemic? Age should not be a consideration in delaying a term insurance plan. Even in the prime of health, there might be accidents or illnesses that may prove fatal. That is why buying a term insurance plan at an early age is better. As you buy the plan, you can get early coverage against life's unpredictable challenges.
2. Affordable Premiums
The premium of a term insurance policy depends on age, besides other factors. They may be affordable for younger individuals and vice-versa. So, when you buy the plan early on, you might save on the premium cost.
3. Tax Benefits1
The premiums paid for term insurance plans qualify for a deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act of 1961. Premiums up to 10% of the sum assured, subject to a maximum of Rs.1.5 lakhs, can be claimed as a deduction under Section 80C, if conditions of section are satisfied. This benefit is available only in case the Old Tax Regime is opted. Further, as announced in the Union Budget 2023$, for life insurance policies (other than ULIPs), issued on or after 1 April 2023 with aggregate annual premium payable of more than Rs. 5 lakhs (excluding GST), proceeds from such policies will be taxable and income from such policies will be treated as ‘Income from Other Sources’. If the aggregate annual premium is less than Rs. 5 lakhs,
income from such policy will continue to be tax free in the hands of policyholder subject to satisfaction of Section 10(10D) conditions.
There is no change in tax treatment for existing policies issued before April 1, 2023. For such polices, a policyholder is entitled to get all the erstwhile tax benefits, as applicable under relevant sections of the Income Tax Act, 1961. The death benefit received under term insurance plan continues to remain tax free2.
When you buy the plan early, you can claim the tax benefits early on and reduce your tax liability.
4. Peace of Mind
Lastly, buying a term insurance plan early also gives you peace of mind. When you have bought a term insurance plan, you know that in the case of an unforeseen contingency, your family would be financially taken care of. This gives you a sense of financial security for your loved ones.
Things to Keep in Mind When Buying A Term Plan
While the aforementioned reasons tell you why you need to consider buying the term plan as soon as possible, there are a few things that you should keep in mind before and after buying the insurance plan. Some of them are as follows –
1. Choose an adequate sum assured which would be sufficient to cover your family’s financial needs
and goals 2. Choose a long-term coverage tenure for longer protection 3. Choose a suitable premium paying term so that the plan becomes affordable 4. Opt for suitable riders to enhance the scope of your coverage, on payment of additional nominal
premium, and make it more inclusive 5. Review your term insurance coverage regularly. If your financial needs increase at the later stages
of life, you might need to supplement an existing term insurance coverage. Some term plans allow enhancement of the sum assured at important milestones of life, like marriage or childbirth. Check whether your plan has this option. If not, or if you need additional sum assured for another reason, you can consider buying another term plan with requisite benefits and coverage.
When it comes to buying term insurance, delay may be avoided. It may be preferred to buy the term insurance plan early on, for financial protection against life’s unplanned emergencies and for financial security of your loved ones.
Reference
1. https://cleartax.in/s/80c-80-deductions
2. https://www.livemint.com/money/personal-finance/new-income-tax-rules-proceeds-
from-life-insurance-policy-to-tax-free-income-changes-from-1st-april-that-you-must- know-11680239868062.html
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility
